scenarios.context:
You are a middle school student in Paris applying to academic-track high schools (lycées) through the centralized admissions process. You must submit a rank-order list of the schools you wish to attend.

You may list between 1 and 8 schools, ranked from most preferred to least preferred. The Paris Education Authority will use your list and your academic grade score to assign you to a school through a deferred acceptance process. Schools admit students in order of their academic grade scores, giving extra priority to low-income students. Once the process is complete, you will be placed at the highest-ranked school on your list that has an available seat for a student with your score.

Your academic grade average: 16.0

Last year's admission cutoffs (minimum grade average admitted) for each school:
  School 1 (most selective): 15.5
  School 2: 14.5
  School 3: 13.8
  School 4: 13.0
  School 5: 12.5
  School 6: 12.0
  School 7: 11.5
  School 8: 11.0
  School 9: 10.5
  School 10: 10.0
  School 11 (least selective): 9.0

Note: If your grade average is below a school's cutoff, that school was fully subscribed last year at a score higher than yours, meaning students with higher scores took all available seats. Students who are not admitted to any listed school are assigned to their neighborhood school.
